product.name: GitHub non-code product platform
product.goal: Reproduce GitHub product semantics for people, enterprises, organizations, teams, repositories, collaboration, engagement, governance, commerce, and integrations without implementing source-code or Git capabilities.

excludedCapabilities.name: git-and-repository-content
excludedCapabilities.reason: Git objects, files, commits, refs, branches, tags, clone, fetch, push, and repository content are outside the product boundary.

excludedCapabilities.name: diff-merge-and-pull-requests
excludedCapabilities.reason: Diffs, mergeability, merge execution, pull requests, reviews, and code-review state require the excluded code domain.

excludedCapabilities.name: actions-and-code-products
excludedCapabilities.reason: Actions execution, code search and navigation, code security analysis, Dependabot, Packages, Pages, and Codespaces are excluded.

deferredCapabilities.name: forks-and-templates
deferredCapabilities.requires: Git history and repository-content provisioning providers.

deferredCapabilities.name: community-profile
deferredCapabilities.requires: A repository-content provider for community files.

deferredCapabilities.name: code-rulesets
deferredCapabilities.requires: Branch, tag, push, and code-governance resources.

deferredCapabilities.name: repository-traffic-metrics
deferredCapabilities.requires: A trustworthy product-telemetry ingestion, retention, and aggregation capability.

contexts.name: accounts
contexts.responsibility: Personal, managed, setup, and bot account identity and lifecycle.

contexts.name: authentication
contexts.responsibility: Credentials, sessions, two-factor authentication, recovery, and external login binding.

contexts.name: profiles
contexts.responsibility: Public and private personal profiles, profile status, and pinned-item presentation.

contexts.name: social-graph
contexts.responsibility: Following relationships between users and organizations.

contexts.name: enterprises
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise identity, profile, account mode, lifecycle, and organization ownership.

contexts.name: enterprise-memberships
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise membership, invitations, affiliation, guest collaborators, and unaffiliated users.

contexts.name: enterprise-teams
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise-wide teams used for centralized role, organization, and license assignment.

contexts.name: enterprise-roles
contexts.responsibility: Predefined and custom enterprise roles, permissions, and assignments.

contexts.name: enterprise-iam
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise identity-provider configuration, SAML or OIDC authentication, SCIM provisioning, and group synchronization.

contexts.name: enterprise-policies
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise policy constraints applied across owned organizations and repositories.

contexts.name: custom-properties
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise-defined repository and organization custom-property schemas, organization values, and promotion of organization repository properties.

publishedEvents.name: EnterpriseRepositoryPropertyPromoted
publishedEvents.meaning: organization repository property promoted to enterprise authority.

contexts.name: organizations
contexts.responsibility: Organization identity, profile, lifecycle, verified domains, and enterprise ownership.

contexts.name: organization-memberships
contexts.responsibility: Organization membership, invitations, member roles, and membership lifecycle.

contexts.name: organization-teams
contexts.responsibility: Organization teams, nested hierarchy, visibility, membership, maintainers, and mentions.

contexts.name: organization-roles
contexts.responsibility: Predefined and custom organization roles and custom repository-role definitions.

contexts.name: organization-policies
contexts.responsibility: Organization policies for repositories, collaborators, projects, discussions, and member privileges.

contexts.name: custom-properties
contexts.responsibility: Organization-defined repository custom-property schemas and repository property values from organization or enterprise definitions.

publishedEvents.name: OrganizationRepositoryPropertyPromotionRequested
publishedEvents.meaning: organization repository property submitted for enterprise promotion.

contexts.name: repositories
contexts.responsibility: Repository identity, personal or organization ownership, name, description, homepage, visibility, lifecycle, redirects, and transfer.

contexts.name: repository-access
contexts.responsibility: Repository invitations, direct and inherited grants, outside collaborators, role assignments, and source-attributed effective permission resolution.

contexts.name: repository-features
contexts.responsibility: Repository Issues, Discussions, and Projects enablement with feature-specific configuration.

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryIssuesFeatureChanged
publishedEvents.meaning: repository Issues enablement or creation policy changed.

contexts.name: repository-metadata
contexts.responsibility: Repository topics and social-media preview configuration.

contexts.name: issues
contexts.responsibility: Issue lifecycle, assignment, hierarchy, dependency, transfer, and work tracking.

contexts.name: issue-schema
contexts.responsibility: Organization-level issue type and field definitions, visibility, pinning, and type-field associations.

contexts.name: labels-and-milestones
contexts.responsibility: Repository-scoped labels, milestones, and work classification.

contexts.name: conversations
contexts.responsibility: Capability-constrained comments, discussion replies, reactions, mentions, revisions, and locks for a closed set of subjects.

contexts.name: discussions
contexts.responsibility: Repository discussion forums and organization discussion spaces, source-repository binding, categories, sections, polls, answers, pins, and lifecycle.

publishedEvents.name: OrganizationDiscussionSpaceEnabled
publishedEvents.meaning: organization discussion space enabled with a source repository.

publishedEvents.name: OrganizationDiscussionSourceChanged
publishedEvents.meaning: organization discussion source repository changed without transferring existing discussions.

contexts.name: moderation
contexts.responsibility: Content reports, moderation cases, blocks, interaction limits, and visibility decisions.

contexts.name: projects
contexts.responsibility: User- or organization-owned projects, items, draft issues, views, fields, workflows, charts, templates, and status updates.

contexts.name: stars
contexts.responsibility: Repository starring and user-defined star lists for discovery and collection.

contexts.name: subscriptions
contexts.responsibility: Repository watch preferences, conversation participation and manual subscriptions, ignore preferences, and notification-interest decisions.

contexts.name: notifications
contexts.responsibility: User notification records, inboxes, reasons, filters, and read, saved, or done state.

publishedEvents.name: NotificationDeliveryRequested
publishedEvents.meaning: notification delivery requested after recipient and read-access resolution.

contexts.name: github-app-registrations
contexts.responsibility: GitHub App registration, ownership and ownership transfer, requested permissions, webhook preference, requested webhook events, and visibility.

publishedEvents.name: GitHubAppDeleted
publishedEvents.meaning: GitHub App registration deleted and its installations invalidated.

contexts.name: github-app-installations
contexts.responsibility: GitHub App installation targets, selected repositories, granted permissions, suspension, and uninstall lifecycle.

contexts.name: oauth-app-registrations
contexts.responsibility: OAuth App registration, ownership, callback configuration, and client lifecycle.

contexts.name: oauth-authorizations
contexts.responsibility: User authorization of registered OAuth Apps, scopes, approval, and revocation.

contexts.name: repository-autolinks
contexts.responsibility: Repository-scoped autolink definitions for external resource references.

contexts.name: webhooks
contexts.responsibility: Repository, organization, and enterprise webhook configuration plus GitHub App webhook projections, deliveries, attempts, and redelivery.

contexts.name: billing
contexts.responsibility: Billing accounts, payment profiles, usage, budgets, cost centers, invoices, and spending allocation.

contexts.name: entitlements
contexts.responsibility: Plans, feature entitlements, licenses, assignments, and usage limits.

contexts.name: audit-logs
contexts.responsibility: Organization and enterprise audit events, scopes, actors, targets, search, export, streaming, and retention policy.

contexts.name: search
contexts.responsibility: Permission-filtered search projections across users, organizations, repositories, issues, discussions, and projects.
contexts.eventRationale: Read-model context consumes versioned events and does not publish product facts.

contexts.name: activity-feed
contexts.responsibility: User-visible dashboard and resource activity projections.
contexts.eventRationale: Read-model context consumes versioned events and does not publish product facts.

contexts.name: repository-insights
contexts.responsibility: Non-code repository engagement trends and integration-health projections.
contexts.eventRationale: Read-model context consumes versioned events and does not publish product facts.

contexts.name: event-publication
contexts.responsibility: Dispatch, leasing, retry, operational idempotency, redelivery, and dead-letter handling for context-owned event envelopes.

contexts.name: search-index
contexts.responsibility: Search document indexing, querying, and index lifecycle adapters.

contexts.name: media-storage
contexts.responsibility: Storage and retrieval of media referenced by product domains.

contexts.name: notification-channels
contexts.responsibility: External email or push delivery adapters for accepted notification delivery requests.

contexts.name: audit-storage
contexts.responsibility: Durable storage, export, and retention enforcement for audit records.
